config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diutamakan tanpa batas, itu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan terhadap orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan pribadi. Dalam praktiknya, masyarakat yang sehat justru terbentuk kalau hak individu dihormati terlebih dahulu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to excuse abuse of minorities and government overreach. Public safety and the common good matter, but they only have legitimacy when they are balanced against constitutional rights, due process, and personal liberty. I also do not buy the idea that a healthier society comes from sacrificing individual rights first; in my view, it is healthier when the rules protect people from the majority as much as they support the majority. If society can override individual rights whenever it claims a collective benefit, then the phrase becomes a blank check for oppression.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat budaya di Indonesia, saya justru khawatir logika “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk menekan yang lemah, minoritas, atau orang yang berbeda pendapat. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, karena tanpa itu masyarakat mudah berubah jadi alat pembenaran bagi penyalahgunaan kuasa. Yang sehat menurut saya bukan mengorbankan individu dulu, melainkan mencari keseimbangan yang adil tanpa menghapus martabat pribadi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual. From a U.S. perspective, that idea has too often been used to justify slavery, segregation, censorship, and other abuses where the majority or the state claimed a noble purpose while trampling minority rights. I am not against the common good, but I believe it only has moral force when it is constrained by due process, constitutional protections, and the principle that people do not lose basic rights just because their views are unpopular. A society is stronger when it protects individuals from collective pressure, not when it asks individuals to surrender first and trust power later.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus mengalahkan hak individu sebagai prinsip utama. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam suara berbeda, menekan minoritas, atau membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa, jadi saya tidak percaya itu aman kalau dijadikan prioritas mutlak. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar dan prosedur yang adil supaya tidak berubah menjadi alat penindasan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individunya, melainkan yang paling mampu melindungi semua orang, termasuk yang tidak populer.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, giving society that kind of blanket priority has repeatedly been a way to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government abuse in the name of order or the common good. Public interests do matter, but they have to be limited by constitutional rights, due process, and protections for unpopular minorities, or else “society” just becomes a convenient slogan for whoever has power. In my view, a strong society is one that can meet collective needs without treating individual rights as expendable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
